text.title: Tagged unions with a literal discriminator
text.summary: Use a Pydantic discriminated union (or JSON-Schema oneOf+discriminator) whenever the model must choose between distinct shapes; the discriminator generated first locks the rest of the branch.

section.title: The rule
section.p: A discriminated union pairs each branch with a unique literal value of one shared field (commonly named `kind`, `type`, or `action`). Constrained decoders compile this into a finite-state grammar: emitting `"kind": "search"` makes only the search branch's fields reachable; the model cannot accidentally produce a `url` while inside a `search` action.

rule.statement: For every output where the model picks one of N distinct shapes, define each shape with a `Literal[...]` discriminator field, combine them with `Annotated[A | B | C, Field(discriminator="kind")]`, and place the discriminator first in each branch model.
rule.rationale: Pydantic v2 emits `oneOf` + `discriminator` in the JSON schema; OpenAI strict mode and Outlines compile this into a grammar that prevents cross-branch field bleed, and parsers can switch on a single key.

example.code:
from typing import Annotated, Literal from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
class SearchAction(BaseModel): kind: Literal["search"] = "search" query: str
class FetchAction(BaseModel): kind: Literal["fetch"] = "fetch" url: str
class FinishAction(BaseModel): kind: Literal["finish"] = "finish" summary: str
class AgentTurn(BaseModel): reasoning: str action: Annotated[ SearchAction | FetchAction | FinishAction, Field(discriminator="kind"), ]
example.note: The `kind` literal is the only field shared across branches; once the model writes it, the rest of the action is locked to one shape.

example.code: class AgentTurn(BaseModel): reasoning: str action_type: str # free text — no grammar constraint query: str | None = None # field bleed url: str | None = None # field bleed summary: str | None = None # field bleed
example.note: The model can emit any subset of optional fields. There is no guarantee that `url` is present when `action_type == "fetch"`, and `query`+`url` may co-occur.

section.reference: Working Pydantic discriminated union for an agent action selector.
JSON-Schema patterns: discriminated unions and pattern matching.
